extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed “By” the Company, Directors, Promoter and subsidiaries from matters “Against” them. I counted only the against rows: criminal proceedings are Company 2, Promoter 34, Directors 0 and Subsidiaries 0; statutory/regulatory proceedings are Company 4, Promoter 1, Directors 0 and Subsidiaries 1; tax proceedings for the Company and subsidiaries are 17 and 2 respectively, excluding the Promoter’s personal tax matters and all “By” rows. The aggregate quantified amount against the Company and subsidiaries is the sum of ₹1,412.72 million and ₹11.38 million, or ₹1,424.10 million = ₹142.41 crore; the contingent-liabilities table states line items as of September 30, 2023, totaling ₹293.13 million = ₹29.313 crore, with the claims-against-company row shown as nil. The company-wide Top 5 customer concentration for Fiscal 2023 is 60.49% of revenue from operations; the other percentages identified as segment-only were not used, and no company-wide Top 10 percentage is disclosed. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
